text: Detect wrapped agent code changes in the watchdog supervisor loop
description: Quickly check packages/wrapped-agents/ for modifications during the supervisor tick (e.g. via git status or file mtimes) and set a dirty flag.

reflections.context: Foundation for self-rebuild/relaunch (5af61b3e): the supervisor must notice when the agent edits wrapped-agents' own source so a stale running build can be rebuilt+relaunched.
reflections.outcome: Added wrapper-watch.ts (mtime-based detection of changed wrapped-agents/*/src .ts since launch, pruning node_modules/dist/client) and WatchdogService.checkWrapperDirty() — throttled (~15s) and latched — called at the top of tick(). Exposed wrapperDirty/changedWrapperFiles/isWrapperDirty() as the seam for the relaunch-prompt and broker-rebuild aims.
reflections.effectiveness: Verified-done: `tsc --noEmit` clean and `npm test` green at 52/52, including 6 new tests covering collection/pruning, baseline (nothing dirty at launch), single-file detection, pruned-dir exclusion, and the WatchdogService latch + throttle behavior.
reflections.lesson: common is a CommonJS package (module:commonjs) — `import.meta.url` fails type-check there; use __dirname (matches session-memory.ts). mtime-since-launch beats `git status` here because dev runs via tsx (no watch) and it catches committed-but-not-rebuilt edits too.
reflections.pattern: Keep detection a pure, separately-tested helper; integrate via a thin throttled/latched method so the supervisor tick stays cheap.

status.comment: Implemented in WatchdogService.checkWrapperDirty() (throttled, latched), called from tick(). New helper module wrapper-watch.ts does mtime-based detection of edits to wrapped-agents/*/src since launch (prunes node_modules/dist/client). Exposes wrapperDirty / changedWrapperFiles / isWrapperDirty() for the relaunch-prompt (310878de) and broker-rebuild (29021d9a) consumers. 6 new tests; common suite 52/52 + type-check clean.
